content: 2024-2025 cutting-edge active learning agent memory systems can be rearchitected around a nested 'agent-within-agent' memory hierarchy, where a secondary lightweight meta-agent embedded within the memory subsystem autonomously selects which new experience traces to retain and prune, instead of relying on fixed static active sampling rules. This meta-agent itself uses curiosity-driven intrinsic reward to prioritize retaining ambiguous, under-learned memories that boost the main agent's downstream task performance the most.
